fair_claims_settlement_practices.regulation_reference: TIC Chapter 542, Subchapter B — Prompt Payment of Claims (§542.051–542.061)
fair_claims_settlement_practices.description: Texas Fair Claims Settlement Practices govern prompt payment and handling of insurance claims.

provisions.title: 15-Day Acknowledgment
provisions.requirement: Insurer shall acknowledge receipt of claim within 15 days.

provisions.title: Acceptance or Denial
provisions.requirement: Insurer shall accept or deny the claim within a reasonable time after receiving required information.

provisions.title: Payment and 18% Penalty
provisions.requirement: Upon acceptance, payment shall be made promptly. Delayed payment beyond statutory period may trigger 18% penalty.

total_loss_regulations.description: Texas total loss threshold and ACV valuation requirements.

provisions.title: Total Loss Threshold
provisions.requirement: Total loss threshold varies by insurer; typically when repair cost exceeds a percentage of ACV.

provisions.title: ACV and Comparables
provisions.requirement: ACV determination must use comparable vehicles; salvage deductions must be disclosed.

provisions.title: Owner Retention
provisions.requirement: If policyholder retains salvage, insurer must disclose deduction and salvage title implications.

time_limits_and_deadlines.description: Critical time limits for Texas auto insurance claims.

deadlines.time_limit: Reasonable time after receiving required information

deadlines.action: Statute of limitations for filing lawsuit
deadlines.time_limit: 2 years personal injury; 2 years property damage; 4 years breach of contract

required_disclosures.description: Mandatory disclosures for Texas auto claims.

disclosures.title: Repair Shop Choice
disclosures.requirement: Insurer must disclose claimant's right to choose repair shop.

disclosures.title: Parts Disclosure
disclosures.requirement: Disclosure of OEM vs aftermarket parts.

disclosures.title: Appraisal Rights
disclosures.requirement: Disclosure of appraisal rights for amount disputes.

uninsured_underinsured_motorist.description: Texas UM/UIM mandatory offer and stacking rules.

provisions.title: Mandatory Offer
provisions.requirement: UM/UIM coverage must be offered. Rejection requires signed form.

provisions.title: Stacking
provisions.requirement: UM setoff rules; stacking governed by TIC §1952.110.

anti_fraud_provisions.regulation_reference: TIC Chapter 701; Texas Penal Code §35.02; TIC §701.051
anti_fraud_provisions.description: Texas insurance fraud and SIU reporting requirements.

provisions.title: Insurance Fraud
provisions.requirement: Insurance fraud is a criminal offense under Texas Penal Code §35.02.

provisions.title: SIU Reporting
provisions.requirement: Insurers must report suspected fraud; SIU requirements under TIC §701.051.

repair_standards.description: Texas repair shop choice, parts, and DRP disclosure requirements.

provisions.title: Shop Choice
provisions.requirement: Claimant has right to choose repair shop. TIC §1952.301–.304.

provisions.title: Parts and Labor Rates
provisions.requirement: 28 TAC §5.4070 governs parts and labor rate surveys.

provisions.title: DRP Disclosure
provisions.requirement: Financial arrangements with DRP shops must be disclosed.

good_faith_requirements.description: Texas unfair claims settlement and bad faith liability.

prohibited_practices.title: Unfair Claims Settlement
prohibited_practices.description: Prohibited unfair claims settlement practices under TIC §541.060.

prohibited_practices.title: Bad Faith Liability
prohibited_practices.description: Bad faith liability exposure under TIC §542.014.

pip_medpay.description: Texas PIP — mandatory offer of $2,500 minimum; rejection requires signed form.

provisions.title: Mandatory Offer
provisions.requirement: PIP must be offered with minimum $2,500. Rejection requires signed form.

subrogation_rules.description: Texas common law: made-whole doctrine not followed — contractual subrogation prevails. TIC §1952.110 (UM setoff).

provisions.title: Contractual Subrogation
provisions.requirement: Texas does not follow made-whole doctrine; contractual subrogation prevails.

provisions.title: Modified Comparative Fault
provisions.requirement: Claimant barred from recovery if 51% or more at fault.

statute_of_limitations.description: Texas statute of limitations for auto claims.
